did_you_know.title: Thurgood Marshall Nominated to the Supreme Court
did_you_know.text: President Lyndon B. Johnson nominated Thurgood Marshall to the U.S. Supreme Court, making him the first African American Justice in the nation's history. Marshall had previously argued Brown v. Board of Education.

did_you_know.title: March on Washington — 'I Have a Dream'
did_you_know.text: Over 250,000 people gathered at the Lincoln Memorial for the March on Washington, where Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his historic 'I Have a Dream' speech — one of the most powerful moments in civil rights history.

did_you_know.title: Jackie Robinson Breaks Baseball's Color Line
did_you_know.text: Jackie Robinson took the field for the Brooklyn Dodgers, becoming the first Black player in Major League Baseball's modern era and permanently changing the face of American sports.
